hub_result.content:
Reading the dispatch command from disk first, then I’ll execute its workflow exactly as written, including the later status, commit, and completion steps.I have the dispatcher workflow loaded. Next I’m reading the current dispatch plan to identify the first eligible non-human worker and its dependency state.The plan has one currently eligible worker: `N-05`. I’m resolving its provider/mode from the routing rules and moving into the spawn/run cycle now.`spawn` succeeded and returned thread `codex_03`. I’m starting `N-05` against the shared agent command file and will classify the result strictly from the JSON response.`N-05` is still executing under the run tool. I’m polling for the structured JSON result rather than inferring state from the partial lifecycle log.The run call itself hasn’t returned yet, so there’s still no JSON result to classify. I’m continuing to wait on the same session rather than restarting or killing it.This is still the original `run` invocation, not a derived non-final state. I’m checking whether the session has completed and emitted the final JSON yet.The lifecycle store shows `N-05` as `🔄`, and there are no new eligible `⬜` rows behind it. I’m staying in the wait state until the run call yields a structured result.The worker has been running for several minutes without the `run` wrapper returning. I’m checking the Meridian roles state files in parallel so I can tell whether this is active progress or a stalled handoff, without mutating anything.The runtime state file shows `N-05` marked `running` on `codex_03`, but there has been no worker heartbeat beyond the start timestamp. I’m reading the local `run` implementation to see whether this wrapper has a built-in timeout or whether the handoff can stall indefinitely.I’ve confirmed the `run` wrapper is using an infinite wait (`sendAndWait(..., 0)`), so a missing callback will hang until interrupted. I’m checking for any local trace or log artifact tied to `codex_03` before deciding whether to escalate this as a dispatcher-level stall.There are fresh file timestamps in the repo from the `N-05` scope, so the worker appears to have edited code even though the `run` wrapper never got a callback. I’m checking for any local log or artifact that would explain whether the worker finished and the callback was dropped.The local logs show repeated attach/detail failures for `codex_03`, including `No registered agent instance found` and earlier hub socket errors. I’m checking the Meridian side now to see whether the hub still has a live agent instance for this thread or whether the dispatcher is waiting on a dead route.I have enough to treat this as a dispatcher stall, not ordinary worker latency: `N-05` is still `🔄`, the `run` wrapper is in an infinite wait, and local logs show repeated `codex_03` attach/registration failures. I’m sending the required human notification on the configured reply channel, then I’ll interrupt the local wait without killing the worker thread.The notification is out. I’m re-reading the plan once to confirm the paused state I’m handing back is still `N-05` in progress, with no newly eligible downstream worker.Dispatch is paused on `N-05`.

I read the dispatcher command, selected the first eligible worker from [dispatch_plan.md](/Users/yzliu/work/Meridian/docs/branch/feat-cli-external-integration/dispatch_plan.md), resolved `CODEX-HIGH -> codex` with `pane_bridge`, spawned thread `codex_03`, and started `run` for `N-05`. The plan still shows `N-05` as `🔄`, and there are no newly eligible downstream workers.

This is a stall, not a normal completion path: the `run` wrapper never returned, and local meridian-roles logs show repeated `codex_03` attach/registration failures, including `No registered agent instance found for thread_id=codex_03`. I sent a high-urgency notify on the configured `web:feat-cli-external` channel asking for intervention and a `resume` or `fail N-05` reply. I did not kill the worker thread.

hub_result.content:
I’m picking up the `R-03` bin registration task and starting by reading the dispatch command so I can follow the branch workflow exactly.I’ve got the dispatch instructions. Next I’m checking pre-flight and the plan row for `R-03`, then I’ll read the task definition and PRD before touching code.The scope is narrow: `package.json`, a shebang check on `src/bin/meridian-tool.ts`, and a compile sanity check. I’m reading those files now so I can patch only what this worker owns.I found the only code change this worker actually needs: register the `bin` in `package.json` and fix the CLI source shebang so the built `dist/bin/meridian-tool.js` is executable under Node. I’m patching just those lines, then I’ll run the task’s compile and shebang checks.The patch is in. I’m verifying the bin registration, the shebang, and the TypeScript build now, and I’ll also check the built CLI help path if the compile succeeds.The compile and build are running cleanly so far. I’m polling for completion, then I’ll confirm the built CLI entry works from `dist/bin/meridian-tool.js`.Registered the CLI bin and fixed the executable shebang for the existing entrypoint. Changes are in [package.json](/Users/yzliu/work/Meridian/Meridian-roles/package.json) and [src/bin/meridian-tool.ts](/Users/yzliu/work/Meridian/Meridian-roles/src/bin/meridian-tool.ts).

Verification passed:
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `npm run build`
- `node dist/bin/meridian-tool.js --help`

`tsconfig.json` already included `src/**/*.ts`, so no compile config change was needed. The built file now starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`, and `package.json` exposes `"meridian-roles": "./dist/bin/meridian-tool.js"` as required.

I did not update the dispatch plan or write the completion report because those paths live under `/Users/yzliu/work/Meridian/docs/...`, which is outside this session’s writable sandbox.
